description: Phase 1.5 integrations & QA bloom: integrations[] (third-party systems with category / ownershipModel) + qualityAttributes seeds.

template_variables.accepted_domains:
- **DOM-URL-SHORTENER**: URL Shortening Engine — Core logic accepting long URLs, validating length (2048 chars), and assigning unique short tokens.
- **DOM-SLUG-GENERATOR**: Unique Slug Logic — Handles collision prevention ensuring global uniqueness for every 6-character token.
- **DOM-REDIRECT-ENGINE**: Redirect Logic — Handles the HTTP 302 response generation and destination lookup.
- **DOM-COUNTER-ANALYTICS**: Click Counter Service — Tracks and increments click counters for every successful redirect request.
- **DOM-STORAGE-ENCRYPT**: Encrypted Storage Layer — Manages encrypted at-rest storage for submitted URLs to treat them as sensitive data.
- **DOM-SECURITY-FILTER**: Threat Detection Gateway — Scans incoming/outgoing URLs for malicious redirects or sensitive content leakage.
- **DOM-API-GATEWAY**: Public API Interface — Manages public access endpoints, authentication bypass, and input sanitization.
- **DOM-RATE-LIMITER**: DoS Protection System — Implements rate limiting to prevent abuse and protect against brute-force attacks.
- **DOM-CACHE-LAYER**: Performance Cache — Stores short-to-medium term redirects to reduce database load and speed up delivery.
- **DOM-AUDIT-LOGGING**: System Audit Logging — Maintains immutable logs of all API calls for debugging and compliance.
- **DOM-BACKUP-OPS**: Backup and Recovery — Handles data redundancy and disaster recovery for link data.
- **DOM-RETENTION-POLICY**: Data Retention Manager — Manages lifecycle of links (deletion policies) to ensure compliance with data privacy.
template_variables.accepted_entities:
- **ENT-LINK** (DOM-URL-SHORTENER): Link Definition — Core entity representing a submitted short link and its target destination.
- **ENT-SLUG-CONFIG** (DOM-SLUG-GENERATOR): Slug Generation Configuration — Defines how unique slugs are generated, including prefix rules and character sets.
- **ENT-SLUG-POOL** (DOM-SLUG-GENERATOR): Slug Pool Registry — Track active slug tokens to prevent collisions and manage expiration.
- **ENT-REDIRECT-CONFIG** (DOM-REDIRECT-ENGINE): Redirect Configuration — Settings controlling HTTP 302 responses and destination validation rules.
- **ENT-REDIRECT-LOG** (DOM-REDIRECT-ENGINE): Redirect Execution Log — Records every successful redirect execution including latency and status.
- **ENT-CLICK-EVENT** (DOM-COUNTER-ANALYTICS): Click Event Record — Individual click tracking for real-time analytics and attribution.
- **ENT-CAMPAIGN** (DOM-COUNTER-ANALYTICS): Marketing Campaign — A group of links tracked together for marketing manager reporting.
- **ENT-CAMPAIGN-STAT** (DOM-COUNTER-ANALYTICS): Campaign Aggregate Statistics — Pre-aggregated metrics for the marketing manager dashboard to improve performance.
- **ENT-IP-GEO-DATA** (DOM-COUNTER-ANALYTICS): IP Geolocation Data — Map IP addresses to geographic regions for analytics visualization.
- **ENT-ENCRYPTED-PAYLOAD** (DOM-STORAGE-ENCRYPT): Encrypted Target Payload — The encrypted version of the original long URL stored at rest.
- **ENT-DECRYPTION-META** (DOM-STORAGE-ENCRYPT): Decryption Metadata — Metadata required to safely decrypt the payload during redirect.
- **ENT-THREAT-INDICATOR** (DOM-SECURITY-FILTER): Threat Indicator Registry — Database of known malicious patterns and IPs to block redirects.
- **ENT-SCAN-RESULT** (DOM-SECURITY-FILTER): Security Scan Result — Result of a security scan for a specific incoming URL.
- **ENT-BLOCKLIST-RULE** (DOM-SECURITY-FILTER): Blocklist Rule — Configuration rules for IP ranges or domains to block.
- **ENT-API-KEY** (DOM-API-GATEWAY): API Access Key — Credentials for external clients to access the public API.
- **ENT-API-CLIENT** (DOM-API-GATEWAY): API Client Profile — Profile of an automated system interacting with the service.
- **ENT-AUTH-TOKEN** (DOM-API-GATEWAY): Authentication Token — Transient token for session management and client validation.
- **ENT-RATE-LIMIT-BUCKET** (DOM-RATE-LIMITER): Rate Limit Bucket — State container for rate limiting per client or IP address.
- **ENT-RATE-LIMIT-CONFIG** (DOM-RATE-LIMITER): Rate Limit Configuration — Thresholds and window sizes for preventing DoS and abuse.
- **ENT-IP-SCORE** (DOM-RATE-LIMITER): IP Reputation Score — Dynamic score of an IP address based on request patterns.
- **ENT-ABUSE-REPORT** (DOM-RATE-LIMITER): Abuse Incident Report — Log of abusive behaviors detected by the rate limiting system.
- **ENT-CACHE-ENTRY** (DOM-CACHE-LAYER): Cache Key Entry — Stored short URL mapping in the performance cache layer.
- **ENT-CACHE-TASK** (DOM-CACHE-LAYER): Cache Management Task — Scheduled job to populate cache or warm specific keys.
- **ENT-CACHE-EVICT-LOG** (DOM-CACHE-LAYER): Cache Eviction Log — Track when and why cache entries were removed for capacity.
- **ENT-AUDIT-RECORD** (DOM-AUDIT-LOGGING): Audit Log Record — Immutable record of API calls, errors, and system events.
- **ENT-AUDIT-INDEX** (DOM-AUDIT-LOGGING): Audit Index Map — Efficient index structure for querying audit logs by date or type.
- **ENT-GDPR-REQUEST** (DOM-AUDIT-LOGGING): GDPR Compliance Request — Record of Data Subject Access Requests for export or deletion.
- **ENT-BACKUP-JOB** (DOM-BACKUP-OPS): Backup Execution Job — Task representing a scheduled or manual backup run.
- **ENT-SNAPSHOT-FILE** (DOM-BACKUP-OPS): Backup Snapshot — Physical or logical container holding the backup data.
- **ENT-RECOVERY-PLAN** (DOM-BACKUP-OPS): Disaster Recovery Plan — Procedure and resource map for restoring service after failure.
- **ENT-POLICY-RULE** (DOM-RETENTION-POLICY): Data Retention Rule — Configuration for how long link data must be kept.
- **ENT-RETENTION-TASK** (DOM-RETENTION-POLICY): Retention Cleanup Task — Automated job to delete expired data per policy rules.
- **ENT-LIFECYCLE-LOG** (DOM-RETENTION-POLICY): Link Lifecycle Event Log — History of a link's life including creation and deletion events.
- **ENT-COMPLIANCE-DATA** (DOM-AUDIT-LOGGING): Compliance Data Set — Structured data specifically formatted for regulatory exports.
- **ENT-SYSTEM-CLOCK** (DOM-AUDIT-LOGGING): System Clock Reference — NTP synchronized time reference for log ordering.
- **ENT-NOTIFICATION-TPL** (DOM-RETENTION-POLICY): Notification Template — Text templates used for expiry notifications to managers.
- **ENT-COMMIT-LOG** (DOM-STORAGE-ENCRYPT): Storage Commit Log — Audit of encryption key rotation and storage integrity checks.
- **ENT-REGION-MAPPING** (DOM-CACHE-LAYER): Region Endpoint Map — Configuration for where cache nodes reside geographically.
- **ENT-HEALTH-METRIC** (DOM-AUDIT-LOGGING): System Health Metric — Current operational status of the infrastructure components.
- **ENT-DISASTER-STATE** (DOM-BACKUP-OPS): Disaster Recovery State — Current state of active disaster recovery or failover.
- **ENT-FEATURE-FLAG** (DOM-RATE-LIMITER): Feature Flag Record — Toggle switches for enabling new features like advanced analytics.
- **ENT-CONFIG-VERSION** (DOM-RETENTION-POLICY): Configuration Version — Snapshot of system configuration at a specific point in time.
- **ENT-METADATA-DEF** (DOM-URL-SHORTENER): Link Metadata Definition — Schema definition for the data fields attached to a link.
- **ENT-REDIRECT-TIMEOUT** (DOM-REDIRECT-ENGINE): Redirect Timeout Record — Logs of redirects that failed due to network latency or timeout.
- **ENT-PAYLOAD-SIZE** (DOM-STORAGE-ENCRYPT): Encrypted Payload Size Tracker — Monitors the size of stored data to enforce storage limits.
template_variables.accepted_personas:
- **P-SHARER**: Link Sharer — Any user needing to share a long URL but wants to minimize space and clutter. Does not require login or persistence.
  Goals: Obtain a short URL for a long target immediately; Ensure the short URL works reliably for others
  Pain points: Long URLs break text layout or UI limits; Uncertainty if a shared link works for recipients
- **P-CLICKER**: Link Clicker — Any user who receives or encounters a short URL. Interaction is passive (click -> redirect).
  Goals: Reach the intended destination instantly; Not be exposed to malicious redirects or broken links
  Pain points: Slow redirects or connection timeouts; Not knowing if the link was clicked or tracked
- **P-API-CLIENT**: Programmatic Client — Automated systems or scripts that interact with the public API to fetch or create links without human intervention.
  Goals: Integrate link shortening into their own workflows; Ensure API availability for their bots
  Pain points: API rate limits blocking their automation; Error handling when links expire
- **P-PLATFORM-OPS**: Platform Operations — Internal team managing the uptime and health of the public service infrastructure.
  Goals: Maintain 99.9% uptime for public redirects; Monitor security logs for threats
  Pain points: Database connection failures; High latency in redirect chains
- **P-MARKETING-MGR**: Marketing Manager — Uses the public shortener for campaign tracking and analytics.
  Goals: Track click volume on specific campaigns; Ensure links do not expire prematurely
  Pain points: Difficulty distinguishing clicks from ads; Confusion over which links drove conversions
template_variables.accepted_journeys:
- **UJ-SUBMIT-NEW-LINK** [Phase 1] User Submits New Short URL Request (P-SHARER): P-SHARER pastes a long URL into the input field and submits a request to create a short link.
- **UJ-CLICK-SHORT-URL** [Phase 1] Clicker Navigates to Short Link (P-CLICKER): P-CLICKER receives a short URL and clicks it to reach the target destination.
- **UJ-REQUEST-QUOTA** [Phase 1] Client Requests Batch Link Creation (P-API-CLIENT): P-API-CLIENT script sends multiple POST requests to the API endpoint.
- **UJ-ENFORCE-RATE-LIMIT** [Phase 1] Client Encounters Rate Limit (P-API-CLIENT): P-API-CLIENT hits DoS protection threshold and receives a 429 response.
- **UJ-REQUEST-GDPR-EXPORT** [Phase 1] Sharer Requests Data Export (P-SHARER): EU-based P-SHARER requests their data under GDPR Right to Access.
- **UJ-REQUEST-PII-DELETION** [Phase 1] Sharer Requests Right to be Forgotten (P-SHARER): P-SHARER invokes deletion right for their specific slug.
- **UJ-MARKET-TRACK-CLICKS** [Phase 1] Manager Views Campaign Analytics (P-MARKETING-MGR): P-MARKETING-MGR accesses analytics dashboard for a specific tracking link.
- **UJ-MARKET-CREATE-CAMPAIGN** [Phase 1] Manager Creates Tracking Link (P-MARKETING-MGR): P-MARKETING-MGR uses the public shortener for campaign tracking.
- **UJ-OPS-MONITOR-HEALTH** [Phase 1] Ops Team Monitors Service Uptime (P-PLATFORM-OPS): P-PLATFORM-OPS reviews system metrics to ensure availability targets.
- **UJ-OPS-TRIGGER-RECOVERY** [Phase 1] Ops Team Initiates Disaster Recovery (P-PLATFORM-OPS): P-PLATFORM-OPS detects regional failure and triggers RTO plan.
- **UJ-HOST-ENFORCE-HTTPS** [Phase 1] System Enforces HTTPS on HTTP Request (P-SHARER): P-SHARER attempts to use HTTP endpoint, system redirects to HTTPS.
- **UJ-SEC-REJECT-MALICIOUS** [Phase 1] System Blocks Malicious URL Submission (P-SHARER): P-SHARER submits a link known or detected to be malicious by security filter.
- **UJ-RETENTION-NOTIFY-EXPIRY** [Phase 1] System Notifies Manager of Retention Policy Expiry (P-MARKETING-MGR): P-MARKETING-MGR receives notice that their tracking link is subject to deletion per policy.
- **UJ-OPS-AUDIT-LOG-REVIEW** [Phase 1] Ops Team Reviews System Audit Logs (P-PLATFORM-OPS): P-PLATFORM-OPS inspects logs for debugging or compliance verification.
- **UJ-SECURITY-ENCRYPT-STORE** [Phase 1] System Encrypts Stored URL at Rest (P-PLATFORM-OPS): System step ensuring compliance with AES-256 storage requirement.
- **UJ-CLICK-FAILURE-TIMEOUT** [Phase 1] Clicker Encounters Redirect Timeout (P-CLICKER): P-CLICKER attempts to access a link that times out during redirect logic.

baseline.response_text:
{
  "kind": "integrations_qa_bloom",
  "integrations": [
    {
      "id": "INT-IDP-AUTH",
      "name": "Identity Provider Integration",
      "category": "identity",
      "description": "Authenticates API clients and manages session tokens.",
      "standardProviders": ["Auth0", "Microsoft Entra"],
      "ownershipModel": "delegated",
      "rationale": "Supports UJ-OPS-TRIGGER-RECOVERY and UJ-SEC-REJECT-MALICIOUS by verifying client identity before granting API access or processing requests.",
      "source": "domain-standard"
    },
    {
      "id": "INT-GEO-LOC",
      "name": "IP Geolocation Service",
      "category": "geocoding",
      "description": "Maps client IP addresses to geographic regions for analytics.",
      "standardProviders": ["MaxMind", "ipinfo"],
      "ownershipModel": "consumed",
      "rationale": "Enables P-MARKETING-MGR to visualize click locations and supports UJ-MARKET-TRACK-CAMPAIGNS.",
      "source": "domain-standard"
    },
    {
      "id": "INT-SEC-THREAT",
      "name": "Threat Intelligence Feed",
      "category": "ai",
      "description": "Real-time database of malicious IPs and URLs to block redirects.",
      "standardProviders": ["VirusTotal", "AbuseIPDB"],
      "ownershipModel": "consumed",
      "rationale": "Critical for UJ-SEC-REJECT-MALICIOUS and DOM-SECURITY-FILTER to prevent phishing and malware distribution.",
      "source": "domain-standard"
    },
    {
      "id": "INT-KMS-KEY",
      "name": "Key Management Service",
      "category": "compliance",
      "description": "Manages encryption keys for data-at-rest encryption.",
      "standardProviders": ["AWS KMS", "HashiCorp Vault"],
      "ownershipModel": "delegated",
      "rationale": "Ensures compliance with DOM-STORAGE-ENCRYPT requirement for AES-256 encryption and key rotation.",
      "source": "domain-standard"
    },
    {
      "id": "INT-OBJ-STORE",
      "name": "Object Storage Backend",
      "category": "storage",
      "description": "Stores encrypted link payloads and metadata.",
      "standardProviders": ["AWS S3", "Google Cloud Storage"],
      "ownershipModel": "delegated",
      "rationale": "Provides durable storage for ENT-ENCRYPTED-PAYLOAD and ENT-ENCRYPTED-PAYLOAD size tracking.",
      "source": "domain-standard"
    },
    {
      "id": "INT-LOG-AGG",
      "name": "Log Aggregation Platform",
      "category": "analytics",
      "description": "Ingests audit logs for analysis and immutable storage.",
      "standardProviders": ["Datadog", "Splunk"],
      "ownershipModel": "delegated",
      "rationale": "Supports P-PLATFORM-OPS UJ-OPS-AUDIT-LOG-REVIEW and GDPR export requests.",
      "source": "domain-standard"
    },
    {
      "id": "INT-DR-BACKUP",
      "name": "Disaster Recovery Storage",
      "category": "storage",
      "description": "Handles redundant backups and point-in-time recovery.",
      "standardProviders": ["AWS Backup", "Veeam"],
      "ownershipModel": "delegated",
      "rationale": "Enables UJ-OPS-TRIGGER-RECOVERY and RTO/RPO compliance.",
      "source": "domain-standard"
    },
    {
      "id": "INT-COM-EMAIL",
      "name": "Email Notification Service",
      "category": "communication",
      "description": "Sends expiry notices and alerts to stakeholders.",
      "standardProviders": ["SendGrid", "Amazon SES"],
      "ownershipModel": "delegated",
      "rationale": "Supports UJ-RETENTION-NOTIFY-EXPIRY for informing Marketing Managers of link deletion.",
      "source": "domain-standard"
    },
    {
      "id": "INT-CMP-WAF",
      "name": "Web Application Firewall",
      "category": "other",
      "description": "Provides DDoS mitigation and rate limiting enforcement.",
      "standardProviders": ["Cloudflare", "AWS Shield"],
      "ownershipModel": "delegated",
      "rationale": "Directly implements DOM-RATE-LIMITER and UJ-ENFORCE-RATE-LIMIT functionality.",
      "source": "domain-standard"
    },
    {
      "id": "INT-SCH-TASK",
      "name": "Task Scheduler",
      "category": "other",
      "description": "Orchestrates background jobs for retention cleanup and cache warming.",
      "standardProviders": ["AWS EventBridge", "Airflow"],
      "ownershipModel": "delegated",
      "rationale": "Executes ENT-RETENTION-TASK and ENT-CACHE-TASK workflows automatically.",
      "source": "domain-standard"
    },
    {
      "id": "INT-MSG-SMS",
      "name": "SMS Alert Provider",
      "category": "messaging",
      "description": "Sends urgent alerts for system downtime or security incidents.",
      "standardProviders": ["Twilio", "AWS SNS"],
      "ownershipModel": "delegated",
      "rationale": "Provides secondary channel for UJ-OPS-MONITOR-HEALTH failures.",
      "source": "ai-proposed"
    },
    {
      "id": "INT-NTP-TIME",
      "name": "Time Sync Service",
      "category": "compliance",
      "description": "Ensures system clocks are accurate for audit log timestamps.",
      "standardProviders": ["Chrony", "NTP Pool"],
      "ownershipModel": "delegated",
      "rationale": "Critical for ENT-AUDIT-INDEX integrity and legal compliance.",
      "source": "domain-standard"
    }
  ],
  "qualityAttributes": [
    "API availability 99.9% measured over rolling 30 days",
    "Redirect response time < 100ms at P99",
    "Audit logs immutable retention 365 days minimum",
    "AES-256 encryption standard for all stored payloads",
    "GDPR data deletion completed within 24 hours",
    "Rate limit enforced at 1000 requests per minute per IP",
    "HSTS headers enabled on all public endpoints",
    "SSL/TLS 1.2 or higher required for all connections",
    "Cache entries expire automatically after 72 hours",
    "Maximum link payload size strictly limited to 2048 bytes",
    "System error rate maintained below 1% uptime",
    "Disaster Recovery Time Objective (RTO) under 4 hours",
    "Cross-region backup replication lag < 1 hour",
    "IP reputation scoring accuracy > 95% on validation queries",
    "Threat indicator blocking latency < 5 seconds",
    "Daily automated backup execution without manual intervention",
    "Monitoring alert notification latency < 1 minute",
    "API JWT token default expiration set to 1 hour",
    "Multi-region failover switch-over < 30 seconds",
    "SOC2 Type II certification status active",
    "Payload compression applied to 80% of storage writes",
    "Database connection pool size adjusted for >500 concurrent users",
    "Cache eviction triggers logged with timestamped reason",
    "Threat feed update frequency < 15 minutes"
  ]
}

IP reputation scoring accuracy > 95% on validation queries
Threat indicator blocking latency < 5 seconds

Monitoring alert notification latency < 1 minute

Multi-region failover switch-over < 30 seconds
SOC2 Type II certification status active
Payload compression applied to 80% of storage writes
Database connection pool size adjusted for >500 concurrent users
Cache eviction triggers logged with timestamped reason
Threat feed update frequency < 15 minutes

baseline.thinking:
The user wants me to propose external system integrations and quality attributes for a "URL Shortener" product (Phase 1 Sub-Phase 1.5).
I need to look at the accepted domains, entities, workflows, and personas to determine what external integrations are necessary.
The JSON output must be strict, with no markdown, no markdown code blocks, no trailing commas, and specific string constraints (straight ASCII quotes, single quotes inside values).

I need to cover:
1.  **Integrations**: Payment, Identity, ERP, Storage, Geocoding, AI/ML, Compliance, Messaging, CRM, Analytics, etc.
2.  **Quality Attributes**: Concrete, testable strings (e.g., "Latency under 50ms for redirect", "99.9% availability").

Let's analyze the domains and entities:
- **DOM-URL-SHORTENER / DOM-SLUG-GENERATOR**: Core functionality.
- **DOM-COUNTER-ANALYTICS**: Needs geolocation, click counting. Integrations: IP Geolocation API, Analytics/Telemetry Platform.
- **DOM-STORAGE-ENCRYPT**: Needs encryption. Integrations: Key Management Service (KMS), Cloud Storage (S3/GCS).
- **DOM-SECURITY-FILTER**: Threat detection. Integrations: Threat Intelligence Feeds (VirusTotal, AbuseIPDB), DNS Blacklists.
- **DOM-API-GATEWAY**: Auth. Integrations: OAuth Provider (Auth0), Identity Provider.
- **DOM-RATE-LIMITER**: DoS protection. Integrations: Cloud WAF (AWS Shield, Cloudflare), CDN.
- **DOM-CACHE-LAYER**: Performance. Integrations: Redis/Memcached managed services, CDNs.
- **DOM-AUDIT-LOGGING**: Logs. Integrations: Log Aggregator (ELK, Splunk, DataDog).
- **DOM-BACKUP-OPS**: DR. Integrations: Backup Vault, Snapshot service (AWS Backup, Azure Backup).
- **DOM-RETENTION-POLICY**: Privacy. Integrations: Compliance APIs (GDPR tools), Data Subject Access Request portal.

Personas/Journeys:
- **P-SHARER**: Needs analytics (optional but nice), encryption, rate limiting.
- **P-CLICKER**: Needs security, geolocation for safety/stats.
- **P-API-CLIENT**: Needs auth, rate limiting.
- **P-PLATFORM-OPS**: Needs monitoring, backup, disaster recovery.
- **P-MARKETING-MGR**: Needs analytics.

External Systems/Integrations to propose:
1.  **Identity/Auth**: Auth0 or Cognito (for API Client auth).
2.  **Geolocation**: MaxMind or ipinfo (for IP to Geo mapping).
3.  **Storage**: AWS S3 or GCS (for encrypted payload storage).
4.  **Cache**: Redis (managed) or Memcached.
5.  **Security**: VirusTotal API or AbuseIPDB for threat indicators.
6.  **Log Aggregation**: Datadog or Splunk.
7.  **Monitoring**: Prometheus/Grafana or Datadog.
8.  **Backup**: AWS Backup or Veeam.
9.  **CDN/WAF**: Cloudflare for DDoS protection and caching.
10. **Email/SMS**: SendGrid or Twilio (for expiry notifications mentioned in UJ-RETENTION-NOTIFY-EXPIRY).
11. **CRM**: Salesforce or HubSpot (if marketing integration is deep, but here it's a public shortener, maybe less relevant unless we support marketing platform integration). Let's stick to core needs first.
12. **AI/ML**: Threat detection model, but the security filter handles this. Maybe an AI-based link classification.
13. **Payment**: Not really needed unless we charge for links (not mentioned). I will omit payment unless standard. I will omit payment as it's a standard URL shortener, not a SaaS billing product.
14. **DNS**: Route53 for traffic routing (part of infrastructure, but could be listed).
15. **DNS Lookup**: To validate destination URLs or check for blacklisted domains.

Let's refine the integrations to match the "Product Integration & Quality Proposer" role for a "URL Shortener".

**Integrations List**:
1.  Identity Provider (Auth0/Cognito) -> Delegated -> API-Gateway auth.
2.  Geolocation Service (ipinfo/MaxMind) -> Consumed -> Analytics (Clicker location).
3.  Managed Cache (Redis) -> Owned/Consumed (often internal, but can be managed service) -> Cache Layer.
4.  Managed Cache (AWS ElastiCache) -> Consumed -> Cache Layer.
5.  Threat Intelligence (VirusTotal/AbuseIPDB) -> Consumed -> Security Filter.
6.  Email Provider (SendGrid) -> Delegated -> Retention Policy Notifications.
7.  Log Aggregation (Datadog/Loki) -> Delegated -> Audit Logging.
8.  Backup Service (AWS Backup) -> Delegated -> Backup Ops.
9.  WAF/CDN (Cloudflare/AWS Shield) -> Delegated -> Rate Limiting/Security.
10. Key Management (AWS KMS/Azure KeyVault) -> Delegated -> Storage Encryption.
11. DNS Resolver (Cloud DNS/Route53) -> Delegated -> Redirect Engine (if needed for custom domains) or just basic resolution.
12. Scheduling (AWS EventBridge/Cron) -> Consumed -> Retention/Cleanup tasks.
13. Object Storage (S3/GCS) -> Delegated -> Encrypted Storage (Payload).
14. Telemetry/Analytics Platform (Snowplow/PostHog) -> Delegated -> Campaign Stats.

**Quality Attributes**:
- Availability (e.g., "API uptime > 99.9%")
- Latency (e.g., "Redirect response time < 100ms")
- Encryption (e.g., "AES-256 encryption for stored payloads")
- Auth (e.g., "All API requests validated against JWT")
- Rate Limiting (e.g., "Max 1000 requests per minute per IP")
- Logging (e.g., "Audit logs immutable for > 1 year")
- GDPR (e.g., "Data deletion within 24 hours of request")
- DR (e.g., "RTO < 4 hours")
- Storage (e.g., "Payload size limit <= 2048 bytes")
- Error Handling (e.g., "Return 5xx for internal server errors")
- SSL/TLS (e.g., "HSTS headers enabled on all redirects")
- Monitoring (e.g., "Alerts triggered for error rate > 1%")
- Scalability (e.g., "Support > 10k QPS")
- Compliance (e.g., "SOC2 Type II certification active")
- Cache TTL (e.g., "Cache entries expire after 30 days")

I need to ensure the JSON is valid. No markdown fences.
The prompt says "No markdown fences — no triple-backticks".
"Response starts with { and ends with }."
